card.title: mazer: cross-viewport UI reliability and layout hardening
card.summary: Coordinate mobile safe-area, shared overlay geometry, and browser layout through one no-overlap contract and proof matrix.
card.objective: Make menu, Options, play, and Pause remain readable and operable across phone, browser, PWA, and restored-window surfaces without per-screenshot offsets.
acceptanceCriteria: Every primary Mazer surface has an owner, supported viewport category, and no-overlap contract.
Mobile and browser layout work can be verified independently and together through a shared matrix.
No completion claim relies on desktop-only proof for a phone or restored-window defect.
Physical-device findings remain open until operator screenshots confirm the deployed behavior.
discoveries: The previous production fix changed small fitting constants while retaining the crowded overlay structure, so the physical iPhone looked nearly unchanged.
An existing server on the default local proof port could serve stale UI; the corrected proof uses an isolated port and an exact Vercel deployment.
nextActions: Review PR #76 on a physical iPhone and attach menu, Options, play, and Pause screenshots plus Smart Steering feel notes.
Keep installed-PWA banding, native browser chrome, and maximize or restore proof open until physical evidence passes.
evidence: Full Mazer verification passed 363 of 363 tests and the production build.
Focused Smart Steering, settings, UI, and scene verification passed 96 of 96 tests.
Vercel preview dpl_GRbdcJ4KYMAvesH5VSRegBWsPvXS passed the exact deployed 393x852 menu, Options, play, Pause, containment, overlap, scroll, button, and console gate.

entry.headline: PR 76 replaces shallow mobile fitting with deployed phone-shape proof
completed: Shortened status, guide, and toggle copy and increased shared row spacing.
Separated Control Style from Smart Steering in Options and Pause.
Proved the exact review deployment across menu, Options, play, Pause, and scroll endpoints.
discovered: Physical-device acceptance remains distinct from deterministic Chromium phone dimensions.
next: Collect operator iPhone screenshots and feel feedback before production promotion or completion.

card.title: mazer: normalize browser UI layout across viewports
card.summary: Normalize responsive browser geometry and preserve only semantic settings so viewport changes cannot replay stale layout.
card.objective: Recompute all menu, Options, play, and Pause geometry from the active content rectangle while preserving explicit player preferences only.
acceptanceCriteria: Layout-derived geometry is never persisted as player preference state.
A recalculated layout cannot overlap, escape the viewport, or hide essential controls.
Normal reload preserves supported semantic settings with safe migration defaults.
Narrow, wide, phone, and restore endpoints have deterministic evidence.
discoveries: Smart Steering is semantic player intent and must persist separately from the Stick or Arrows control style.
Vercel share credentials are consumed before application load and must not be treated as route drift by the visual proof contract.
nextActions: Complete exact deployed phone-shape proof for the new semantic settings and overlay layout.
Retain native browser chrome and maximize or restore as separate remaining evidence.
evidence: Focused UI and control packet passed before review deployment.

entry.headline: Browser layout and semantic-control correction implemented
completed: Added independently persisted Smart Steering and Control Style fields.
Reworked shared status, guide, toggle-row, and scroll geometry.
Updated runtime diagnostics and the surface capture contract.
discovered: The default proof port was already occupied, so isolated-port and deployed-URL correlation became mandatory.
next: Run the exact deployed review gate and move the card to Review if it passes.

card.title: mazer: shared menu and play run status panel
card.summary: Use one responsive menu and play status component for time, Rank or AI Rank, Score out of 100, and Maze level from the correct context.
card.objective: Give menu and active play the same compact status language while keeping score authority traceable to the canonical scorer.
acceptanceCriteria: Menu and play consume the same status-panel formatting and layout contract.
Panel text remains centered, contained, and readable at phone and desktop widths.
Panel width, gap, and play Pause placement satisfy shared viewport diagnostics.
Displayed score is traceable to canonical run-quality data rather than a presentation-only calculation.
discoveries: The previous compact status strings were too long for physical phone chrome despite passing shallow fit constants.
The presentation slice can advance independently while canonical score authority remains open.
nextActions: Review the PR #76 menu and play status treatment on a physical phone and reconcile final row grouping with the frozen interpretation.
Identify and consume the one finalized canonical 0 to 100 scorer field before completion.
evidence: Mazer draft PR #76 at head 34524bd9 uses one compact menu and play formatter.
Exact deployed 393x852 menu and play text-containment proof passed.
Canonical score authority remains explicitly non-terminal.

entry.headline: Shared run-status presentation advanced without claiming score authority
completed: Shortened Time, Rank, Score, and Maze copy through one menu and play formatter.
Increased safe padding and proved phone-width containment on the exact preview.
discovered: Physical-phone row grouping and canonical score ownership remain acceptance work.
next: Use operator screenshots to settle presentation, then continue the scorer-owned dependency separately.
